title: Write decimals as fractions
description: Practice writing decimals as fractions. These problems use decimals with tenths and hundredths.
topic_path: Get ready for numbers and operations

hints:
Let's look at $0.1$ on a place value table.

Ones |. | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  
$0$ | .| $\blueD1$ |
The number $\blueD1$ is in the tenths place, so we have one tenth.

$0.1$ can be written as $\dfrac{\blueD1}{10}$.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

Let's look at $0.01$ on a place value table.

Ones |. | Tenths | Hundredths
:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:  
$0$ | .| $\blueD0$ | $\purpleC1$

We have a $\blueD0$ in the tenths place.

We can write zero tenths as $\dfrac{\blueD0}{10}$.

We have a $\purpleC1$ in the hundredths place.

We can write one hundredth as $\dfrac{\purpleC1}{100}$.

Add the two parts together.

 $\dfrac{\blueD0}{10} + \dfrac{\purpleC1}{100}$
